20.reason: Array size is too small (10) for input with 15 line segments, causing stack smashing

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Array size is too small (10) for input with 15 line segments, causing stack smashing
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Array size is too small (10) for input with 15 segments, causing stack smashing
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Array size too small for n > 10; causes buffer overflow (stack smashing)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Array size 10 is too small; can hold at most 10 segments but input may have up to 1000, causing stack smashing.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The array size is fixed at 10, but the input can have up to 1000 segments (as seen in the reference program and test case with 15 segments). This causes a buffer overflow when n > 10, leading to a Runtime Error (stack smashing). It should be increased to accommodate the maximum possible input size, e.g., line a[1000]; or dynamically allocated.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The array size is fixed at 10, but the input can have up to 15 (or more) segments as seen in the test case. This causes a buffer overflow when reading input, leading to stack smashing/runtime error. It should be large enough to handle the maximum constraint (e.g., 1000).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The array size is too small. The test case has 15 segments, but the array can only hold 10, causing buffer overflow and stack smashing.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The array size is too small. The test case has 15 segments, but the array can only hold 10, causing buffer overflow and stack smashing.]

24.reason: Loop uses n to index a[] which is too small; out-of-bounds access when n > 10.
